name: Project Infi Root Law
principle: No action becomes state unless it is verified, judged, debt-reckoned, truthfully admitted, stabilized, and bound.

config_unification_law.name: Config Unification Law
config_unification_law.principle: Everything must be unified. Nothing may conflict. One canonical source per concern; all loaders resolve through the governance registry.
forbidden_examples: duplicate greeting or confirm phrase definitions across configs

master_boot_law.name: Master Boot Law
master_boot_law.principle: One master boot ISO remasters from metal baseline with unified governance, full runtime stack, and fail-closed preflight.

daily_driver_law.name: Daily Driver Law
daily_driver_law.principle: Real daily use absorbs worldwide OS pain — Linux, Windows, macOS — in silent infrastructure. Governance speaks only when something actually matters.

operator_trust_policy_law.name: Operator Trust Policy Law
operator_trust_policy_law.principle: Governance without operator trust is governance beating a dead horse. Hard law blocks real danger; operator trust reduces friction and keeps receipts.

operator_pattern_law.name: Operator Pattern Evidence Law
operator_pattern_law.principle: When the operator detects an artifact mismatch, boot anomaly, size drift, kernel panic, or behavior that does not match known-good reality, the system must treat that observation as first-class verification evidence.
operator_pattern_law.release_rule: No ISO is promoted when operator pattern evidence conflicts with tool-only success.

runtime_weight_law.name: Runtime Update Weight Law
runtime_weight_law.principle: Every runtime patch or upgrade must verify against the original metal boot reference before weight is recorded; weight is tracked after each patch and upgrade.
